title: Sending bulk email campaigns

posts.author: wRgRJapyPWPsdhyMs98f (James)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all

I have, what i deem to be a very simple problem, but i am having a lot of trouble finding any kind of reference to it online.

I am a new user and have a list of prospects (200+) that i would like to send a marketing message to, how do i do this…?

It will be a “simple text” message, perhaps with a link - and all i want to do is batch together the contacts and send the same message to the list.

I have integrated mailchimp and set up an account but there doesn’t appear to be any kind of two way integration (not a simple one anyway)

Am i missing something super obvious here, it feels like it

Thanks in advance

James

posts.author: ajwhitman (AJ W)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m having a similar issue - we have a Mailchimp account integrated as well, but we use that more for newsletters and marketing campaigns, and I want a simple way to email all the people listed in a certain “group” with a much simpler message than a whole formatted campaign.

Our use case: We have a Mailchimp list with a whole bunch of people on it, including volunteers, donors, clients, etc. who get our newsletter. But I want a way to email the volunteers in a certain group, to say for example “we need X volunteers for Y activity, reply if interested.”

So far the only ways I can think to do this are either through Mailchimp, even though that’s not really the best tool for this use-case, or to make a separate Google Group or something.

posts.cookedHtml:
I’m have the same problem/question. The article you pointed us to tells us how to integrate email but it doesn’t address the issue of sending bulk emails. So, if I filter out 20 people I want to send an email to, how do I do that?

Thanks!

posts.author: grootwassink (Dave Grootwassink)
posts.cookedHtml:
Not going to fork over more money for a premium level, given lack of basic features. I may well be dropping the whole thing next renewal time.

If there are technical people on here looking for this, I solved it this way:

1 - Create a field in the Leads table. You can be creative here, using a status field that can be used to indicate which message to send.
2 - Create bulk email as HTML file.
3 - Write a short piece of Python code that queries the Leads table through the API.
- Select email address and indicated bulk email to send (plus other items you want to include)
- Send bulk message through the API of your email provider.

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @wRgRJapyPWPsdhyMs98f,

You’re not missing anything obvious - monday CRM isn’t really built for sending bulk marketing emails natively, which is why it feels a bit clunky.

A common setup is to send the campaign via Mailchimp (or another email tool) and then keep monday as the system of record. To avoid losing context, an app like Email to Task for monday.com can help by automatically capturing incoming replies and turning them into items or updates on the right prospect, so follow-ups don’t fall through the cracks.

That way:

  • Mailchimp handles the batch send
  • monday tracks engagement and next actions

There’s a 14-day trial, and you can also book a quick demo if you want to see how that flow could work for your list.
